findings.title: Suspicious Port 4444 Traffic — 10.0.0.5 → 192.168.1.50
findings.description: Traffic on port 4444 (Metasploit default listener) detected between 10.0.0.5 and 192.168.1.50.
findings.recommendation: Investigate port 4444 traffic immediately. Isolate hosts and perform forensic analysis. Block at firewall if not legitimate.

findings.title: Port Scan Detected — 192.168.1.20
findings.description: 192.168.1.20 probed 129 distinct destination ports, consistent with automated scanning.
findings.recommendation: Block source IP at perimeter. Enable IPS port-scan signatures. Investigate host for compromise.

findings.title: ARP Spoofing / Cache Poisoning — 192.168.1.1
findings.description: IP 192.168.1.1 appeared with 2 different MAC addresses in ARP replies.
findings.recommendation: Enable Dynamic ARP Inspection (DAI). Add static ARP entries for critical hosts. Investigate for active MITM attack.

findings.title: Cleartext Password in HTTP POST — 192.168.1.80 → 10.0.0.1
findings.description: HTTP payload from 192.168.1.80 contains a password field sent in cleartext.
findings.recommendation: Enforce HTTPS with HSTS. Audit all web forms for cleartext submission.

findings.title: DNS Tunneling Suspected — 192.168.1.40
findings.description: 192.168.1.40 issued 85 DNS queries; 85 had names >50 chars (100%), indicating possible DNS tunneling.
findings.recommendation: Deploy DNS monitoring/filtering. Block excessive or long-name queries. Investigate host for dns2tcp, iodine, or dnscat tools.

findings.title: Beaconing Detected — 192.168.1.10->93.184.216.34:443
findings.description: Regular 0.1s ± 0.27s intervals over 40 packets — consistent with automated C2 heartbeat.
findings.recommendation: Isolate beaconing host immediately. Perform memory forensics. Block C2 destination at firewall/proxy.

findings.title: Beaconing Detected — 192.168.1.60->185.220.101.1:8080
findings.description: Regular 60.0s ± 0.00s intervals over 20 packets — consistent with automated C2 heartbeat.
findings.recommendation: Isolate beaconing host immediately. Perform memory forensics. Block C2 destination at firewall/proxy.

findings.title: Beaconing Detected — 10.0.0.99->192.168.1.10:22
findings.description: Regular 0.5s ± 0.50s intervals over 45 packets — consistent with automated C2 heartbeat.
findings.recommendation: Isolate beaconing host immediately. Perform memory forensics. Block C2 destination at firewall/proxy.

findings.title: Suspicious Port 23 Traffic — 192.168.1.20 → 192.168.1.1
findings.description: Traffic on port 23 (Telnet – cleartext) detected between 192.168.1.20 and 192.168.1.1.
findings.recommendation: Investigate port 23 traffic immediately. Isolate hosts and perform forensic analysis. Block at firewall if not legitimate.

findings.title: Suspicious Port 23 Traffic — 192.168.1.30 → 192.168.1.1
findings.description: Traffic on port 23 (Telnet – cleartext) detected between 192.168.1.30 and 192.168.1.1.
findings.recommendation: Investigate port 23 traffic immediately. Isolate hosts and perform forensic analysis. Block at firewall if not legitimate.

findings.title: Cleartext Protocol: FTP from 192.168.1.20
findings.description: FTP (port 21) is unencrypted. Credentials transmitted are visible to anyone on the path.
findings.recommendation: Replace FTP with its encrypted equivalent (SFTP/SSH/HTTPS/IMAPS/LDAPS). Block plaintext variant at firewall.

findings.title: Cleartext Protocol: Telnet from 192.168.1.20
findings.description: Telnet (port 23) is unencrypted. Credentials transmitted are visible to anyone on the path.
findings.recommendation: Replace Telnet with its encrypted equivalent (SFTP/SSH/HTTPS/IMAPS/LDAPS). Block plaintext variant at firewall.

findings.title: SSH Brute Force — 10.0.0.99->192.168.1.10:22
findings.description: 45 connection attempts to SSH (port 22) from 10.0.0.99.
findings.recommendation: Block source IP. Enable account lockout on SSH. Deploy fail2ban. Use key-based authentication where possible.

findings.title: Suspicious Port 69 Traffic — 192.168.1.20 → 192.168.1.1
findings.description: Traffic on port 69 (TFTP – no auth) detected between 192.168.1.20 and 192.168.1.1.
findings.recommendation: Investigate port 69 traffic immediately. Isolate hosts and perform forensic analysis. Block at firewall if not legitimate.

findings.title: High Outbound Volume — 192.168.1.70
findings.description: 192.168.1.70 sent 12.2 MB outbound, exceeding the 10 MB threshold.
findings.recommendation: Implement DLP controls. Review outbound firewall rules. Correlate with user activity logs.
